Petition To Approve Compromise (Ryan Reedy, Jr.)
Off Calendar Jan-14-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Minor's Compromise.
Department 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6840.

Douglas A. Rochen, Esq., appearing for Petitioner: Kansas Reedy, mother and guardian ad litem for Minor: Ryan Reedy, Jr. present in court.
Petitioner sworn and examined.

After testimony and consideration of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved as follows:
Total Settlement: $10,000.00
Attorney's Fees: $0
Costs: $0
A net balance of $10,000.00 to be deposited into a purchased annuity at:
Pacific Life Insurance Company.

Court signed Order Granting approval of Minor and Order to Deposit Money into a purchased annuity.
Status Hearing Scheduled on April 14, 2016 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514 for filing of receipt for the deposit of money into a purchased annuity.
No appearance is required if the receipt is filed and courtesy copy lodged with Dept. 514 prior to Hearing.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DOUGLAS A. ROCHEN, ESQ. of Kabateck Brown Kellner LLP, Tel No. 213-217-5000 appearing telephonically for Plaintiffs;
Atty. RYAN D. MARSHALL, ESQ. of McCormick Barstow LLP, Tel No. 559-433-1300 appearing for Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Mark Rieser are overruled. The grounds for the objections go to the weight of the evidence, not its admissibility. Mr. Rieser's declaration shows that there are triable issues precluding summary judgment including whether the pole should have been: 1) placed farther away from the edge of the roadway, 2) shielded in some fashion, and 3) had additional or better markers or strips of some fashion to make the pole more visible to drivers. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
